frameworks.name: size constancy
frameworks.one_line: perceptual compensation mapping retinal size to real-world size via recent context

frameworks.name: population receptive field modeling
frameworks.one_line: voxel-wise tuning estimation for topographic object-size maps in parietal cortex

frameworks.name: central tendency / serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: perceptual biases toward stimulus-set mean and previous trial

core_question: Does the range of recently viewed object sizes bias both perceived object size and its parietal neural representation?
key_assumptions: size perception integrates context of recent retinal sizes
parietal object-size maps reflect perceptual rather than purely retinal size
pRF tuning can index size preference and width
manipulations: presented object size range (small 0.05-1.4 deg vs large 0.1-2.8 deg)
trial history (previous trial size for serial dependence)
key_findings: central tendency: perceived sizes attracted toward across-trial mean
serial dependence: perceived size attracted toward previous trial's size
parietal size preferences and tuning widths follow presented range
neural changes are smaller than changes in presented object sizes
interpretations: size constancy uses recent context to bias both perception and parietal coding
parietal object-size maps are perceptual, not strictly retinotopic
